market_direction.summary: Cross-market mobility can unlock upside, but demand conversion remains uneven across geographies.
evidence: International transition paths perform best when prior platform signals are explicit.
Domestic continuity paths still show steadier near-term conversion in many sectors.

competition_pressure.summary: Competition pressure is elevated in transition-heavy paths requiring market revalidation.
evidence: Cross-border repositioning introduces parallel local and returnee competition.
Portability claims without market-specific proof increase screening loss.

economic_pressure.summary: Economic pressure is moderate: cautious budgets persist, while selected strategic hiring continues.
evidence: Regional volatility drives selective hiring pace rather than full freeze.
Employers favor tighter mandate fit under uncertain demand signals.

transition_friction.summary: Transition friction is elevated where relocation and family constraints interact within the same window.
evidence: Schooling and housing constraints increase execution burden in cross-border timing windows.
Role conversion and relocation frictions can compound when sequencing discipline is weak.

source_notes: Perplexity Sonar synthesis with cross-market labor and macro updates.
Comparative interpretation constrained to AMC external-layer contract v1.
